questions.q: What is the right to equality of opportunity?
opts: (C) Right to equality in all walks of life
questions.explanation: Equality of opportunity means equal chance to everyone in the country to get a job or get education, or to participate in the political process.

questions.q: Who is entitled to equality of opportunity?
opts: (D) Only citizens of a particular state
questions.explanation: Equality of opportunity is a fundamental right of all citizens of a country, as stated in the Constitution.

questions.q: What is the purpose of equal pay for equal work?
opts: (C) To ensure equal pay for men and women
questions.explanation: Equal pay for equal work is a principle that ensures that men and women receive the same pay for doing the same work.

questions.q: What is the right to freedom of speech and expression?
opts: (B) Right to speak against the government
(D) Right to speak only in a particular language
questions.explanation: Freedom of speech and expression is a fundamental right that allows individuals to express their opinions freely without fear of censorship or reprisal.

questions.q: Who is entitled to freedom of speech and expression?
questions.explanation: Freedom of speech and expression is a universal right that applies to all individuals, not just citizens or men.

questions.q: What is the purpose of freedom of assembly?
opts: (B) To ensure freedom of speech and expression
(C) To allow individuals to assemble peacefully
questions.explanation: Freedom of assembly allows individuals to come together and express their opinions peacefully, without fear of persecution or reprisal.

questions.q: What is the right to life and personal liberty?
opts: (A) Right to die at any age
(B) Right to live at any cost
(C) Right to live with dignity and freedom
questions.explanation: The right to life and personal liberty is a fundamental right that ensures that individuals have the right to live with dignity and freedom.

questions.q: Who is entitled to the right to life and personal liberty?
questions.explanation: The right to life and personal liberty is a universal right that applies to all individuals, not just citizens or men.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the right to freedom from torture?
opts: (B) To ensure freedom of speech and expression
(C) To prevent physical and mental harm
questions.explanation: The right to freedom from torture ensures that individuals are protected from physical and mental harm, and are treated with dignity and respect.

questions.q: What is the right to freedom from slavery and forced labor?
opts: (B) Right to be forced to work
(C) Right to be free from slavery and forced labor
questions.explanation: The right to freedom from slavery and forced labor ensures that individuals are protected from exploitation and are treated with dignity and respect.

questions.q: Who is entitled to the right to freedom from slavery and forced labor?
questions.explanation: The right to freedom from slavery and forced labor is a universal right that applies to all individuals, not just citizens or men.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the right to freedom from exploitation?
opts: (B) To ensure freedom of speech and expression
questions.explanation: The right to freedom from exploitation ensures that individuals are protected from exploitation and are treated with dignity and respect.

questions.q: What is the right to freedom of movement?
opts: (A) Right to remain in one place
(C) Right to move freely within the country
questions.explanation: The right to freedom of movement ensures that individuals have the freedom to move within the country without any restrictions.

questions.q: Who is entitled to the right to freedom of movement?
questions.explanation: The right to freedom of movement is a universal right that applies to all individuals, not just citizens or men.

questions.q: What is the purpose of freedom of residence?
opts: (B) To ensure freedom of speech and expression
(C) To allow individuals to reside anywhere
questions.explanation: Freedom of residence allows individuals to reside anywhere within the country without any restrictions.

questions.q: What is the right to freedom from arbitrary arrest and detention?
opts: (A) Right to be arrested at any time
(B) Right to be detained without trial
(C) Right to be free from arbitrary arrest and detention
questions.explanation: The right to freedom from arbitrary arrest and detention ensures that individuals are protected from arbitrary arrest and detention, and are treated with dignity and respect.

questions.q: Who is entitled to the right to freedom from arbitrary arrest and detention?
questions.explanation: The right to freedom from arbitrary arrest and detention is a universal right that applies to all individuals, not just citizens or men.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the right to a fair trial?
opts: (B) To ensure freedom of speech and expression
questions.explanation: The right to a fair trial ensures that individuals are treated fairly and are protected from arbitrary arrest and detention.

questions.q: What is the right to protection of life and liberty?
opts: (A) Right to risk life and liberty
(B) Right to protect life and liberty at any cost
(C) Right to protect life and liberty
(D) Right to sacrifice life and liberty
questions.explanation: The right to protection of life and liberty ensures that individuals are protected from harm and are treated with dignity and respect.

questions.q: Who is entitled to the right to protection of life and liberty?
questions.explanation: The right to protection of life and liberty is a universal right that applies to all individuals, not just citizens or men.
